public int countGoodStrings(...) {

   int mod = 1e9+7;

   int[] dp = new int[high + 1];

       dp[i] = (int) (a % mod);

   }

   int total = 0;

   for (int l = low; l <= high; l++) {

       total = (total + dp[l]) % mod;

   }

   return total;

}

class Solution {
    public int countGoodStrings(int low, int high, int zero, int one) {
        int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[] dp = new int[high + 1];
        dp[0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= high; i++) {
            int count = 0;
            if (i >= zero) count += dp[i - zero];
            if (i >= one) count += dp[i - one];
            dp[i] = count % MOD;
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int l = low; l <= high; l++) {
            result = (result + dp[l]) % MOD;
        }
        return result;
    }
}

class Solution {
    public int countGoodStrings(int low, int high, int zero, int one) {
        int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[] dp = new int[high + 1];
        dp[0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= high; i++) {

            if (i >= zero) count += dp[i - zero];
            if (i >= one) count += dp[i - one];
            dp[i] = count % MOD;

        int result = 0;
        for (int l = low; l <= high; l++) {
            result = (result + dp[l]) % MOD;
        }
        return result;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countGoodStrings(int low, int high, int zero, int one) {
        int MOD = 1000000007;
        int[] dp = new int[high + 1];
        dp[0] = 1;
        for (int i = 1; i <= high; i++) {
            int count = 0;
            if (i >= zero) count += dp[i - zero];
            if (i >= one) count += dp[i - one];
            dp[i] = count % MOD;
        }
        int result = 0;
        for (int l = low; l <= high; l++) {
            result = (result + dp[l]) % MOD;
        }
        return result;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(high)
space complexity: O(high)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses dynamic programming to count the number of ways to construct strings of each length up to high. For each length i, it sums possibilities from adding zero or one. Sums results for lengths in [low, high]. Efficient for constraints but uses linear space.
